translatedText: Electromagnetism
Established electromagnetism by providing a unified foundation for electric and magnetic phenomena, and proved that light is an electromagnetic wave.
Maxwell's Equations
Mathematically formulated the existing theories of electromagnetism into Maxwell's equations, a set of differential equations that form the foundation of electromagnetism.
Kinetic Theory of Gases
Formulated the velocity distribution law of gas molecules and laid the foundation for statistical mechanics by suggesting probabilistic concepts.
Color Theory and Color Photography
Produced the first color photograph in 1861 by applying the principle that all colors can be expressed by mixing the three primary colors.
Research on Saturn's Rings
Showed that Saturn's rings must consist of numerous small particles to be stable, a prediction proved by the Voyager spacecraft in the 1980s.
Who is James Clerk Maxwell?
A British theoretical physicist and mathematician born in Edinburgh, Scotland, considered the founder of electromagnetism for providing a unified foundation for electric and magnetic phenomena.
What is Maxwell's most important achievement?
Providing a unified foundation for electric and magnetic phenomena, establishing electromagnetism, and mathematically presenting 'Maxwell's equations'.
What contribution did Maxwell make to color photography?
He created the first color photograph in 1861 by applying the principle that all colors can be represented by a mixture of three primary colors.
When and where was Maxwell born, and when did he die?
He was born on June 13, 1831, in Edinburgh, Scotland, and died of stomach cancer in Cambridge on November 5, 1879, at the age of 48.
Birth of Moctezuma II
Succeeded his uncle Ahuitzotl to become the ninth Huey Tlatoani of the Aztec Empire
Ruled the Aztec Empire
Death
The male line of Moctezuma became extinct when his last direct male descendant, Seino de Moctezuma, died in New Orleans, USA
The Count of Miravalle, a descendant of Moctezuma, demanded that the Mexican government reinstate the 'Moctezuma pension'
Rule of the Aztec Empire
Ruled as the ninth Huey Tlatoani of the Aztec Empire from 1502 to 1520.
Territorial Expansion
During his reign, the Aztec Empire reached its maximal extent, expanding its territory to present-day Honduras and Nicaragua.
Attempts to Strengthen Centralization
Abolished the Jaguar and Eagle warrior systems in an attempt to strengthen centralization.
Response to Spanish Conquistador Hernán Cortés
Attempted to bribe Hernán Cortés and lured him to the capital Tenochtitlan, but was taken prisoner.
When did Moctezuma II rule the Aztec Empire?
Moctezuma II ruled as the ninth Huey Tlatoani of the Aztec Empire from 1502 to 1520.
What important events occurred during the reign of Moctezuma II?
During his reign, the Spanish began their conquest of the Aztecs in earnest, and the empire's territory grew to its largest size, stretching to present-day Honduras and Nicaragua.
How did Moctezuma II die?
According to Spanish records, he died on June 29, 1520, three days after being injured by stones and arrows while attempting to address his subjects.
Are there any direct male descendants of Moctezuma II?
The last direct male descendant died in 1836, ending the male line, but descendants through his daughter Tecuichpoch still exist today.
Tran Hung Dao, a royal family member and general of the Tran dynasty of Dai Viet, was born
When an envoy of the Mongol general Uriyangkhadai demanded free passage through Vietnam, Tran Hung Dao was tasked with defending the border
When Kublai Khan's son Toghon invaded Vietnam with an army of 500,000, Emperor Nhan Tong appointed Tran Hung Dao as Commander-in-Chief to lead the army
The army led by Tran Hung Dao fought against the Mongol forces, but the capital Thang Long fell, and the Retired Emperor and Emperor Nhan Tong fled to Thanh Hoa
Tran Hung Dao compiled the 'Summary of Military Tactics' and wrote the 'Proclamation to the Officers' to boost the morale of his soldiers
June 1285
The army led by Tran Hung Dao recaptured Thang Long and forced Toghon's Mongol army to withdraw
Late 1287
When the Yuan army invaded again, Tran Hung Dao withdrew his forces from Thang Long and annihilated the Yuan fleet on the Bach Dang River (Battle of Bach Dang)
Tran Hung Dao died, leaving his last words: 'Unite the army like parents and children, treat the people with tolerance, and achieve great deeds with that strength'
Repelling the Mongol Invasions
Successfully repelled two large-scale invasions by the Yuan dynasty in 1285 and 1287, defending Vietnam's independence.
Military Strategy and Morale Boosting
Authored the 'Summary of Military Tactics' and the 'Proclamation to the Officers' to boost soldiers' morale and provide effective military strategies.
Victory at the Battle of Bach Dang
Achieved a decisive victory in the Battle of Bach Dang in 1287 by planting stakes in the riverbed to annihilate the Yuan fleet.
What was Tran Hung Dao's real name?
Tran Hung Dao's real name was Tran Quoc Tuan, and Hung Dao is a title bestowed upon him later to praise his achievements.
What books did Tran Hung Dao author?
His works include the 'Summary of Military Tactics' (Binh Thu Yeu Luoc) and the 'Van Kiep Secret Manual' (Van Kiep Tong Bi Truyen Thu).
What is a famous statement made by Tran Hung Dao?
During the Mongol invasion of 1285, he showed his will to fight by stating, 'If Your Majesty wishes to surrender, please have my head cut off first.'
Around 240
Born in Palmyra, Syria, which was under Roman rule.
Around 255
Became the second wife of Odaenathus, the ruler of Palmyra, at the age of 14.
Became the Empress of the Palmyrene Empire when Odaenathus was crowned Emperor.
After the assassination of Emperor Odaenathus, she appointed her son Vaballathus as his successor and became the co-ruler of the Palmyrene Empire.
Declared the Palmyrene Empire an independent state and decided to expand its territory.
The Palmyrene army, led by General Zabdas, conquered Egypt and Syria.
October 270
The Palmyrene army killed the Roman prefect of Egypt.
Mid-271
Expanded territory as the Palmyrene Empire conquered Anatolia and Palestine.
Roman Emperor Aurelian decided to conquer the Palmyrene Empire and invaded with his army.
The Roman Empire conquered the Palmyrene Empire.
Brought as a captive in Emperor Aurelian's triumphal procession in Rome.
Expansion of the Palmyrene Empire
Expanded the territory of the Palmyrene Empire to Syria, Anatolia, Palestine, and Egypt.
Cultural Development of the Palmyrene Empire
Fostered the culture of the Palmyrene Empire by opening the court to scholars and philosophers and implementing a policy of tolerance toward Christians and Jews.
Declaration of Independence and Rule of the Palmyrene Empire
Declared the Palmyrene Empire an independent state in 270 and led the empire as co-ruler.
When was Zenobia born?
Zenobia was born around 240 in the ancient city of Palmyra in Syria, which was under Roman rule.
What was Zenobia's family life like?
Around 255, at age 14, she became the second wife of Odaenathus, the ruler of Palmyra, and they had three sons: Vaballathus, Hairan II, and Septimius Antiochus.
What languages did Zenobia speak?
From a young age, Zenobia spoke Egyptian, Latin, Greek, and Syriac.
How did Zenobia become the ruler of the Palmyrene Empire?
After the assassination of Emperor Odaenathus in 267, she appointed her son Vaballathus as his successor and became co-ruler of the Palmyrene Empire.
What happened to Zenobia in the end?
She was paraded as a captive in Emperor Aurelian's triumphal procession in Rome in 274, but her exact fate after that remains unclear.
August 23, 1769
Born in Montbéliard
Death
Drafting the Classification of the Animal Kingdom
Drafted a classification chart dividing the animal kingdom into 4 branches and 15 classes.
Founding Paleontology
Investigated fossils through comparative anatomical studies and founded paleontology.
Advocating Catastrophism
Denied Lamarck's theory of evolution, insisted on the fixity of species, and advocated 'catastrophism'.
Who was Georges Cuvier?
He was a French zoologist and statesman.
What were Georges Cuvier's main academic achievements?
He created a classification system dividing the animal kingdom into 4 branches and 15 classes, and founded paleontology through his comparative anatomical studies.
What theory did Georges Cuvier advocate?
He denied Lamarck's theory of evolution, insisted on the fixity of species, and proposed the theory of 'catastrophism'.
Where was Georges Cuvier born?
He was born in Montbéliard.
How did Georges Cuvier become a professor at the Paris Museum?
He became a professor at the Paris Museum upon the recommendation of Saint-Hilaire.
What does Hatshepsut's name mean?
The name Hatshepsut means 'Foremost of Noble Ladies'.
Who did Hatshepsut marry?
Hatshepsut married her half-brother, Thutmose II.
How is Hatshepsut's reign evaluated?
Egyptologists consider Hatshepsut's reign to be one of the most successful periods among all the Pharaohs.
Who is presumed to have destroyed Hatshepsut's monuments?
It is currently presumed that Amenhotep II destroyed Hatshepsut's monuments.
535 BC
Birth of Heraclitus
Around 504 BC
Entered his floruit (in his 40s) according to Diogenes Laërtius
475 BC
Death of Heraclitus
Nickname 'The Obscure'
He was famous in antiquity as 'the obscure philosopher' (Skoteinos) because he often wrote texts that were difficult to understand.
The Doctrine of Universal Flux
Famous for his saying, 'No man ever steps in the same river twice', he believed that nothing is stable or remains the same.
